# Bondage Source Map

This file contains the programmatic surface (structs, enums, functions, methods, traits) of the **Bondage** library and its **Rope** CLI companion.

---

## Library Core

### [[src/lib.rs]]
Key Symbols: [ToolCall](src/lib.rs), [ToolResponse](src/lib.rs), [Message](src/lib.rs), [ToolDefinition](src/lib.rs), [BondageError](src/lib.rs), [step](src/lib.rs), [step_stream](src/lib.rs)

```rust
// Represents an individual invocation request generated by the language model.
struct ToolCall {
    id: String,
    name: String,
    arguments: String,
}

// Stores the outcome of a tool execution to be sent back to the model.
struct ToolResponse {
    id: String,
    name: String,
    content: String,
    is_error: bool,
}

// Defines a conversation event that can be sent to or received from the API.
enum Message {
    System(String),
    User(String),
    ModelText(String),
    ModelToolRequest(ToolCall),
    ToolResponse(ToolResponse),
    Error(String),
}

// Declares the schema and usage details of a tool made available to the model.
struct ToolDefinition {
    name: String,
    description: String,
    parameters: serde_json::Value,
}

// Classifies failures that can occur during engine interaction, tool running, or data encoding.
enum BondageError {
    Engine(String),
    Tool(String),
    Io(String),
    Serialization(String),
}

// Executes a single conversational turn with the language model, automatically registering schemas and collecting results.
pub async fn step(
    client: &GenaiClient,
    model: &str,
    messages: &[Message],
    allowed_tools: &[ToolDefinition],
    options: Option<ChatOptions>,
) -> Result<Vec<Message>, BondageError>;

// Processes a conversational turn asynchronously, yielding text segments incrementally via a callback before finalizing the messages.
pub async fn step_stream(
    client: &GenaiClient,
    model: &str,
    messages: &[Message],
    allowed_tools: &[ToolDefinition],
    options: Option<ChatOptions>,
    on_token: &(dyn Fn(String) + Send + Sync),
) -> Result<Vec<Message>, BondageError>;
```

### [src/kit.rs](src/kit.rs)
Key Symbols: [ToolExecutor](src/kit.rs), [step_agent](src/kit.rs), [stdin_ask_approval](src/kit.rs), [DefaultExecutor](src/kit.rs)

```rust
pub trait ToolExecutor {
    // Executes a specific tool request after validating it against user preferences and rules.
    async fn execute(
        &self,
        call: &ToolCall,
        policy: &Policy,
        current_dir: &Path,
    ) -> ToolResponse;
}

// Drives a multi-turn agent loop that repeatedly queries the LLM and executes its requested tools until completion.
pub async fn step_agent(
    client: &genai::Client,
    model: &str,
    history: &mut Vec<Message>,
    tools: &[ToolDefinition],
    options: Option<ChatOptions>,
    current_dir: &Path,
    policy: &Policy,
    executor: &dyn ToolExecutor,
    on_token: &(dyn Fn(String) + Send + Sync),
) -> Result<(), BondageError>;

// Prompts a terminal operator for consent before running a potentially destructive tool.
fn stdin_ask_approval(tool_name: &str, args: &str) -> bool;

// Implements basic tool execution by matching target paths and prompting on the standard input when necessary.
struct DefaultExecutor {}

impl ToolExecutor for DefaultExecutor {
    // Processes the tool call using standard rules and stdin confirmation prompts.
    async fn execute(
        &self,
        call: &ToolCall,
        policy: &Policy,
        current_dir: &Path,
    ) -> ToolResponse;
}
```

### [src/policy.rs](src/policy.rs)
Key Symbols: [PolicyMode](src/policy.rs), [PolicyConfig](src/policy.rs), [Policy](src/policy.rs), [is_path_inside_base](src/policy.rs)

```rust
// Dictates the approval action to take when a tool execution is requested.
enum PolicyMode {
    Yes,
    No,
    Ask,
}

// Holds optional configurations for system action permissions.
struct PolicyConfig {
    access_lookup_directory: Option<PolicyMode>,
    access_lookup_fs: Option<PolicyMode>,
    access_lookup_web: Option<PolicyMode>,
    access_write_directory: Option<PolicyMode>,
    access_write_fs: Option<PolicyMode>,
    access_bash: Option<PolicyMode>,
}

// Represents the concrete security permissions active for the current agent session.
struct Policy {
    lookup_directory: PolicyMode,
    lookup_fs: PolicyMode,
    lookup_web: PolicyMode,
    write_directory: PolicyMode,
    write_fs: PolicyMode,
    bash: PolicyMode,
}

impl Policy {
    // Populates a complete policy by overriding defaults with values supplied in a configuration.
    pub fn from_config(cfg: &PolicyConfig) -> Self;

    // Evaluates whether reading a path or web address complies with the configured security rules.
    pub fn check_lookup(&self, target: &str, base_dir: &Path) -> PolicyMode;

    // Determines the safety level of creating or updating a file at a specific path.
    pub fn check_write(&self, target_path: &str, base_dir: &Path) -> PolicyMode;

    // Retrieves the permission mode currently governing shell execution command runs.
    pub fn check_bash(&self) -> PolicyMode;
}

// Confirms if a canonicalized file location resides strictly within a canonicalized root directory.
fn is_path_inside_base(base_dir: &Path, resolved_path: &Path) -> bool;
```

### [src/prompt_file_injector.rs](src/prompt_file_injector.rs)
Key Symbols: [process_prompt](src/prompt_file_injector.rs), [process_prompt_in_dir](src/prompt_file_injector.rs), [find_file_references](src/prompt_file_injector.rs)

```rust
// Analyzes the prompt for @-prefixed resource markers using the current working directory to locate and inline their contents.
pub fn process_prompt(prompt: &str) -> Result<String, Vec<String>>;

// Scans and replaces resource references recursively up to one level deep under the specified directory path.
pub fn process_prompt_in_dir(prompt: &str, base_dir: &Path) -> Result<String, Vec<String>>;

// Extracts valid file paths marked with a leading symbol and yields any errors for invalid references.
fn find_file_references(text: &str, base_dir: &Path) -> (Vec<PathBuf>, Vec<String>);
```

### [src/tools.rs](src/tools.rs)
Key Symbols: [execute_tool](src/tools.rs), [get_standard_tools](src/tools.rs)

```rust
// Dispatches a tool request to the appropriate execution module by decoding arguments and wrapping outcomes.
pub async fn execute_tool(
    id: &str,
    name: &str,
    arguments: &str,
    base_dir: &Path,
) -> ToolResponse;

// Compiles the definitions and JSON schemas of all standard tools supported by the agent framework.
pub fn get_standard_tools() -> Vec<ToolDefinition>;
```

### [src/util.rs](src/util.rs)
Key Symbols: [to_genai_messages](src/util.rs), [to_genai_tools](src/util.rs), [from_genai_content](src/util.rs), [from_genai_response](src/util.rs), [truncate_text](src/util.rs), [format_tools_block](src/util.rs), [locate_resource](src/util.rs), [expand_tilde](src/util.rs), [resolve_path](src/util.rs)

```rust
// Maps custom messages to the format expected by the underlying genai library, grouping consecutive tool requests.
pub fn to_genai_messages(messages: &[Message]) -> Vec<ChatMessage>;

// Converts tool definitions into the metadata schemas required for LLM tool binding.
pub fn to_genai_tools(tools: &[ToolDefinition]) -> Vec<Tool>;

// Extracts message chunks and tool invocations from genai library response parts into custom messages.
pub fn from_genai_content(content: MessageContent) -> Vec<Message>;

// Simplifies API response structures by extracting their inner contents into a message vector.
pub fn from_genai_response(res: ChatResponse) -> Vec<Message>;

// Shortens long text outputs by preserving only specified boundaries and indicating the omitted length.
pub fn truncate_text(
    content: &str,
    head_lines: usize,
    tail_lines: usize,
    head_chars: usize,
    tail_chars: usize,
) -> String;

// Generates a bulleted markdown string listing names and descriptions of available capabilities.
pub fn format_tools_block(tools: &[ToolDefinition]) -> String;

// Finds files in either local paths or configuration folders based on request criteria.
pub fn locate_resource(
    name: &str,
    is_explicit: bool,
    base_dir: &Path,
) -> Result<PathBuf, String>;

// Resolves home directory reference prefix shortcuts to real system paths.
pub fn expand_tilde(path: &Path) -> PathBuf;

// Computes an absolute path representation, resolving relative components against a working directory.
pub fn resolve_path(base_dir: &Path, path_str: &str) -> PathBuf;
```

## Tool Implementations

### [src/tools/tool_bash.rs](src/tools/tool_bash.rs)
Key Symbols: [BashArgs](src/tools/tool_bash.rs), [definition](src/tools/tool_bash.rs), [execute](src/tools/tool_bash.rs)

```rust
// Holds the shell command string passed to the bash execution tool.
pub struct BashArgs {
    pub command: String,
}

// Declares the metadata and JSON schema requirements for the bash tool.
pub fn definition() -> ToolDefinition;

// Asynchronously runs the command inside a non-interactive bash process and returns formatted outputs.
pub async fn execute(args: BashArgs, base_dir: &std::path::Path) -> Result<String, BondageError>;
```

### [src/tools/tool_lookup.rs](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs)
Key Symbols: [LookupArgs](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs), [resolve_path](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs), [html_escape](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs), [lookup_file](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs), [format_size](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs), [lookup_dir](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs), [lookup_url](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs), [execute](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs), [definition](src/tools/tool_lookup.rs)

```rust
// Specifies the options for searching or reading local directories, local files, and web pages.
pub struct LookupArgs {
    pub target: String,
    pub query: Option<String>,
    pub radius: Option<usize>,
}

// Combines the base directory and the target path string into an absolute path, expanding any tildes.
fn resolve_path(base_dir: &Path, path_str: &str) -> PathBuf;

// Translates special characters like angle brackets into safe XML entities.
fn html_escape(input: &str) -> String;

// Reads a local file's content and extracts matching lines or displays a truncated preview.
fn lookup_file(path: &Path, query: Option<&str>, radius: usize) -> Result<String, BondageError>;

// Converts a raw byte count into a human-readable size string with appropriate binary suffixes.
fn format_size(bytes: u64) -> String;

// Lists directory entries or recursively searches all files in a folder for a query.
fn lookup_dir(path: &Path, query: Option<&str>, radius: usize) -> Result<String, BondageError>;

// Fetches the response from a URL using an HTTP client and scans the body for queries.
async fn lookup_url(url: &str, query: Option<&str>) -> Result<String, BondageError>;

// Dispatches the lookup request to local file reader, directory scanner, or web fetcher based on target type.
pub async fn execute(args: LookupArgs, base_dir: &Path) -> Result<String, BondageError>;

// Generates the JSON schema and description metadata for registerable tool integration.
pub fn definition() -> ToolDefinition;
```

### [src/tools/tool_write.rs](src/tools/tool_write.rs)
Key Symbols: [WriteArgs](src/tools/tool_write.rs), [resolve_path](src/tools/tool_write.rs), [execute](src/tools/tool_write.rs), [definition](src/tools/tool_write.rs)

```rust
// Defines the arguments required to overwrite or patch a file's content.
pub struct WriteArgs {
    pub path: String,
    pub new_content: String,
    pub old_content: Option<String>,
}

// Computes the absolute file path by prepending a base directory and expanding tildes.
fn resolve_path(base_dir: &Path, path_str: &str) -> PathBuf;

// Writes content to the specified path, replacing a specific block if old_content is provided, or over-writing the file.
pub async fn execute(args: WriteArgs, base_dir: &Path) -> Result<String, BondageError>;

// Configures the JSON schema and description metadata for registerable tool integration.
pub fn definition() -> ToolDefinition;
```

## Rope CLI Integration

### [src/rope/config.rs](src/rope/config.rs)
Key Symbols: [Config](src/rope/config.rs), [load_config](src/rope/config.rs), [ensure_resources_installed](src/rope/config.rs)

```rust
struct Config {
    model: Option<String>,
    api_key: Option<String>,
    endpoint: Option<String>,
    adapter: Option<String>,
    terminal: Option<String>,
    policy: bondage::policy::PolicyConfig,
}

impl Config {
    // Merges non-empty override fields from another configuration instance into the current configuration.
    fn merge(&mut self, other: Config) -> ();
}

// Parses a TOML configuration file if it exists, falling back to default values on failure.
fn load_config(config_path: &Path) -> Config;

// Generates directories and writes default configuration/prompt files to the user's home configuration directory if they do not exist.
fn ensure_resources_installed() -> std::io::Result<()>;
```

### [src/rope/executor.rs](src/rope/executor.rs)
Key Symbols: [RopeExecutor](src/rope/executor.rs)

```rust
struct RopeExecutor {
    terminal: Option<String>,
    session_file: Option<PathBuf>,
}

impl ToolExecutor for RopeExecutor {
    // Evaluates tool invocation permissions against the user policy and runs the tool if approved or auto-approved.
    async fn execute(&self, call: &bondage::ToolCall, policy: &bondage::policy::Policy, current_dir: &Path) -> ToolResponse;
}
```

### [src/rope/interactive.rs](src/rope/interactive.rs)
Key Symbols: [content_hash](src/rope/interactive.rs), [has_rope_trigger](src/rope/interactive.rs), [run_file_sitter](src/rope/interactive.rs), [run_agent_turn](src/rope/interactive.rs)

```rust
// Computes a fast hash of the file contents to track changes.
fn content_hash(content: &str) -> u64;

// Verifies if the trigger string is present in the text and isolated by whitespace boundaries.
fn has_rope_trigger(content: &str) -> bool;

// Monitors the target file for modifications to trigger agent execution when the activation string is found.
async fn run_file_sitter(session_file_path: PathBuf, config: Config, client: genai::Client, model: String, system_prompt_template: String) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>;

// Formulates the session state, user request, and system prompts before invoking the agent step loop.
async fn run_agent_turn(session_file: &Path, file_content: &str, config: &Config, client: &genai::Client, model: &str, system_prompt_template: &str) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>;
```

fn make_rope_skin() -> MadSkin;

// Prompts the user on the console to authorize the execution of a given tool and its arguments.
pub fn ask_approval(tool_name: &str, args: &str) -> bool;

// Writes a timed entry to the debug log file if execution tracking is turned on.
fn log_debug(msg: &str) -> ();

// Executes a shell command within a tmux environment under a specified safety policy, optionally popping a terminal window.
pub async fn execute_bash_tmux(
    id: &str,
    arguments: &str,
    current_dir: &std::path::Path,
    policy_mode: bondage::policy::PolicyMode,

### [src/rope/tmux_utils.rs](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs)
Key Symbols: [TerminalHandle](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [is_tmux_available](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [has_session](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [start_session](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [send_command_literal](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [send_control_key](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [get_pane_content](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [is_pane_idle](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [kill_session](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [has_attached_clients](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [pop_terminal](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [close_terminal](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs), [get_pane_cursor_state](src/rope/tmux_utils.rs)

```rust
// Represents a handle to a popped terminal interface, either a GUI window process or a tmux TTY split pane.
pub enum TerminalHandle {
    Gui(Child),
    Tty(String),
}

// Verifies if the tmux executable is present on the system path and functional.
pub fn is_tmux_available() -> bool;

// Determines whether a tmux session with the given name currently exists.
pub fn has_session(session_name: &str) -> bool;

// Initializes a new detached tmux session starting in the designated directory.
pub fn start_session(session_name: &str, current_dir: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()>;

// Sends raw text keys to a target tmux session without executing the line.
pub fn send_command_literal(session_name: &str, command: &str) -> std::io::Result<()>;

// Transmits a key combination, such as Enter or Ctrl+C, to a target tmux session.
pub fn send_control_key(session_name: &str, key: &str) -> std::io::Result<()>;

// Retrieves the full scrollback buffer text from the active pane of a session.
pub fn get_pane_content(session_name: &str) -> std::io::Result<String>;

// Checks whether the command running inside the pane corresponds to an idle shell.
pub fn is_pane_idle(session_name: &str) -> std::io::Result<bool>;

// Destroys the specified tmux session by sending a termination command.
pub fn kill_session(session_name: &str) -> std::io::Result<()>;

// Queries the tmux session to verify if any graphical or console clients are connected.
pub fn has_attached_clients(session_name: &str) -> bool;

// Spawns a terminal window or splits the active tmux pane to display the session interactively.
pub fn pop_terminal(session_name: &str, custom_terminal: Option<&str>) -> std::io::Result<Option<TerminalHandle>>;

// Closes the interactive interface handle by killing the window process or removing the split pane.
pub fn close_terminal(handle: TerminalHandle) -> std::io::Result<()>;

// Measures the current size of the scrollback buffer alongside the vertical cursor position.
pub fn get_pane_cursor_state(session_name: &str) -> std::io::Result<(usize, usize)>;
```
